title: POST /api/admin/digest — daily-cron operator summary email + housekeeping piggyback
problem: Pages Functions don't have scheduled triggers natively. A free external cron (cron-job.org, a CF Workers scheduled trigger in a separate worker) hits this endpoint once a day to do two things: (1) compile a digest email for Marc — any triage rows over 48h old + any unresolved admin_alerts (Stripe-notification fallbacks from the webhook's maybeNotifyAdmin) — and (2) piggyback four housekeeping sweeps that need a daily trigger: prune expired magic-link tokens, cancel stale pending Stripe payments, GC orphan attachment uploads (kind != 'napkin'), and run the email outbox (AUDIT P1.3 — retry pending durable sends, alert on rows that JUST hit OUTBOX_MAX_ATTEMPTS, prune delivered rows past OUTBOX_DELIVERED_TTL_SECONDS). Marc's daily operational alarm without paying for a hosted scheduler.
analysis: POST only. Auth: requires X-Digest-Token header matching env.DIGEST_TOKEN — out-of-band so no human cookie is required (a cron job posts without a browser). CSRF-exempt in middleware. Idempotent — calling twice in the same hour just sends two emails (acceptable; the external cron typically fires once daily). Body: query D1 for triage sessions WHERE updated_at < now() - 48h AND deleted_at IS NULL; query admin_alerts WHERE resolved_at IS NULL. Build the email; send via Resend to primaryAdminEmail (functions/_lib/sessions.ts) in the right lang (getLang(adminEmail)). 200 ok on send, 500 on Resend failure.

decisions.rationale: The caller is a cron job, not a browser. No cookie context, so the standard auth doesn't apply. A shared-secret header is the simplest auth for server-to-server calls; the token lives in CF secrets and the cron job's config. Compromise on either side means rotating the token.
decisions.recommendation: Keep the token-header pattern. If a second cron-triggered endpoint is added, reuse the same secret name + header.

decisions.decision: admin_alerts are surfaced but have no UI to mark resolved
decisions.rationale: admin_alerts rows are inserted when the webhook's maybeNotifyAdmin can't reach Marc via the primary channel (Resend down, etc.) — rare but real. The digest re-surfaces them so they don't vanish. There's no UI to mark resolved because the volume is so low (single-digit per year). Marc manually clears resolved entries in D1 when fixing the underlying issue.
decisions.recommendation: Don't build a UI. If volume crosses ~10/year, evaluate.

successCriteria:
- X-Digest-Token validates against env.DIGEST_TOKEN; mismatch → 401
- Triage rows older than 48h are listed in the email
- Unresolved admin_alerts are listed in the email
- Idempotent — twice-fired produces two emails, no DB mutation
- Send via Resend; failures return 500 (cron should retry)
- 200 + summary on success
implementation: functions/api/admin/digest.ts — onRequestPost. DigestEnv extends Env with DIGEST_TOKEN. Two D1 queries. Email template lives near the handler. Tests: functions/api/admin/digest.test.ts covers auth + content shape.
userGuide: Configure an external cron (cron-job.org, free tier) to POST https://marcportal.com/api/admin/digest with X-Digest-Token header set to the secret. Daily cadence is the intent; hourly would be too noisy. To manually trigger: curl with the header. To inspect last sent: there's no record (the email IS the record); Resend's dashboard has the sent log.
knownLimitations: External cron is a separate failure mode — if cron-job.org is down, no digest. Mitigation: receive emails for active sessions via /api/sessions's notification side effects
Digest content is hand-curated in the handler; a new alert type requires code change
Idempotency is a feature for the visitor's mailbox (no duplicate emails on cron retry); but it ALSO means we can't deduplicate alerts across multiple sends
No quiet hours; cron fires whenever cron-job.org fires

statusHistory.reason: Endpoint shipped with the daily-digest content + DIGEST_TOKEN auth.
Code-first doc co-located. External-cron + token-header pattern stable.

AUDIT P1.3: digest now also sweeps email_outbox (retry pending sends, alert on hard-failed rows, prune delivered ones past TTL). Touches sweepEmailOutbox in functions/_lib/email.ts; needs a re-freeze after the first production sweep validates the cadence.

revisions.author: marc+claude
revisions.reason: AUDIT P1.3 — email_outbox sweep added to the cron's housekeeping. Sweeper retries pending durable sends, fires sendAdminAlert on rows that JUST hit OUTBOX_MAX_ATTEMPTS, and DELETEs delivered rows past OUTBOX_DELIVERED_TTL_SECONDS.
